cues.text: Find a comfortable position. Close the eyes. Let the breath begin.
Start with connected breathing. Inhale flows into exhale, no pause.
Deepen the inhale. The exhale is a relaxed release, not a push.
Let the breath become circular. In and out, like a wave.
If tingling or lightheadedness arises — that's normal. Keep breathing.
Breathe into the heart space. Let it open.
Whatever comes up, let it move through you. The breath carries it.
Stay connected. Don't analyze — just breathe and feel.
You may feel energy, emotion, memories. Welcome them all.
The breath is the teacher. Surrender to it.
If your body wants to move, let it. Trust the process.
Halfway point. You're doing beautifully. Stay with the breath.
Let go even more. There's nothing to hold onto.
The breath may want to shift. Follow it wherever it goes.
If tears come, let them flow. If laughter comes, let it ring.
Begin to gently slow the breath. Not yet — but soon.
Soften the inhale. Lengthen the exhale. Come back slowly.
Return to natural breathing. No effort. Rest now.
Integration. Feel the body from head to toe. You are here.
When you're ready, gently wiggle the fingers and toes.
Take your time. Open the eyes when you feel complete.
